name: AtomicDoubleArray
javadoc:
/** * A {@code double} array in which elements may be updated atomically. * See the {@link java.util.concurrent.atomic} package specification * for description of the properties of atomic variables. * *

This class compares primitive {@code double} * values in methods such as {@link #compareAndSet} by comparing their * bitwise representation using {@link Double#doubleToRawLongBits}, * which differs from both the primitive double {@code ==} operator * and from {@link Double#equals}, as if implemented by: * 

 {@code
 * static boolean bitEquals(double x, double y) {
 *   long xBits = Double.doubleToRawLongBits(x);
 *   long yBits = Double.doubleToRawLongBits(y);
 *   return xBits == yBits;
 * }}
* * @author Doug Lea * @author Martin Buchholz * @since 11.0 */

members.name: compareAndSet
members.javadoc: /** * Atomically sets the element at position {@code i} to the given * updated value * if the current value is bitwise equal * to the expected value. * * @param i the index * @param expect the expected value * @param update the new value * @return true if successful. False return indicates that * the actual value was not equal to the expected value. */

tags.name: expect
tags.assertion: assert args[1] instanceof java.lang.Double && args[1] != null;
tags.description: expect must be of type double and not null

tags.name: update
tags.assertion: assert args[2] instanceof java.lang.Double && args[2] != null;
tags.description: update must be of type double and not null

tags.content:
true if successful. False return indicates that
the actual value was not equal to the expected value.
tags.description: true if successful. False return indicates that the actual value was not equal to the expected value.

members.code: /** * Atomically sets the element at position {@code i} to the given * updated value * if the current value is bitwise equal * to the expected value. * * @param i the index * @param expect the expected value * @param update the new value * @return true if successful. False return indicates that * the actual value was not equal to the expected value. */ public final boolean compareAndSet(int i, double expect, double update) { return longs.compareAndSet(i, doubleToRawLongBits(expect), doubleToRawLongBits(update)); }

members.name: weakCompareAndSet
members.javadoc:
/** * Atomically sets the element at position {@code i} to the given * updated value * if the current value is bitwise equal * to the expected value. * *

May * fail spuriously * and does not provide ordering guarantees, so is only rarely an * appropriate alternative to {@code compareAndSet}. * * @param i the index * @param expect the expected value * @param update the new value * @return true if successful */
members.code:
/** * Atomically sets the element at position {@code i} to the given * updated value * if the current value is bitwise equal * to the expected value. * *

May * fail spuriously * and does not provide ordering guarantees, so is only rarely an * appropriate alternative to {@code compareAndSet}. * * @param i the index * @param expect the expected value * @param update the new value * @return true if successful */ public final boolean weakCompareAndSet(int i, double expect, double update) { return longs.weakCompareAndSet(i, doubleToRawLongBits(expect), doubleToRawLongBits(update)); }

true if the previous value was equal to the sum of the current element and the delta.
